name: crypto

metadata.name: crypto
metadata.summary: Simple symmetric GPG file encryption and decryption

documentation:
Documentation: <http://chrissimpkins.github.io/crypto/>

# Description

crypto provides a simple interface to symmetric Gnu Privacy Guard (gpg) encryption and decryption for one or more files on Unix and Linux platforms. It runs on top of gpg and requires a gpg install on your system. Encryption is performed with the AES256 cipher algorithm. [Benchmarks relative to default gpg settings are available for text and binary file mime types](https://chrissimpkins.github.io/crypto/benchmarks.html).

crypto provides a number of options including automated tar archives of multiple files prior to encryption, portable ASCII armored encryption formatting, and SHA256 hash digest generation for your encrypted files. You can view all available options in the [usage documentation](http://chrissimpkins.github.io/crypto/usage.html) or with the `--help` option.

Tested in cPython 2.7.x, 3.4.x, and pypy 2.6.x (Python version 2.7.9)

# Install

Install with `pip` using the command:

```bash
$ pip install crypto
```

or [download the source repository](https://github.com/chrissimpkins/crypto/tarball/master), unpack it, and navigate to the top level of the repository. Then enter:

```bash
$ python setup.py install
```

# Upgrade

You can upgrade your crypto version with the command:

```bash
$ pip install --upgrade crypto
```

# Usage

## Encryption (crypto)

```bash
$ crypto <options> [file path] <file path 2...>
```

```bash
$ crypto <options> [directory path] <directory path 2...>
```

## Decryption (decrypto)

```bash
$ decrypto <options> [file path] <file path 2...>
```

```bash
$ decrypto <options> [directory path] <directory path 2...>
```

You can find all available options in the [documentation](http://chrissimpkins.github.io/crypto/usage.html) or by using one of the following commands:

```bash
$ crypto --help
$ decrypto --help
```

# Frequently Asked Questions

[FAQ link](http://chrissimpkins.github.io/crypto/faq.html)

# Issue Reporting

Issue reporting is available on the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/chrissimpkins/crypto/issues)

functions.name: crypto.library.package.generate_tar_files
documentation: Public function that reads a list of local directories and generates tar archives from them

functions.name: crypto.library.package.remove_tar_files
documentation: Public function that removes temporary tar archive files in a local directory

functions.name: crypto.library.package._generate_tar
documentation: Private function that reads a local directory and generates a tar archive from it

functions.name: crypto.library.hash.generate_hash
documentation: Public function that reads a local file and generates a SHA256 hash digest for it

functions.name: crypto.library.hash._calculate_sha256
documentation: Private function that calculates a SHA256 hash digest for a binary string argument

classes.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor

methods.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor.encrypt_file
documentation: public method for single file encryption with optional compression, ASCII armored formatting, and file hash digest generation

methods.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor.encrypt_files
documentation: public method for multiple file encryption with optional compression, ASCII armored formatting, and file hash digest generation

methods.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor.cleanup
documentation: public method that overwrites user passphrase in memory
cleanup : overwrite the passphrase in memory

methods.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor._create_outfilepath
documentation: private method that generates the crypto saved file path string with a .crypt file type

methods.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor._is_compress_filetype
documentation: private method that performs magic number and size check on file to determine whether to compress the file

methods.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor._is_common_binary
documentation: private method to compare file path mime type to common binary file types

methods.name: crypto.library.cryptor.Cryptor._is_common_text
documentation: private method to compare file path mime type to common text file types
performs gpg encryption of one or more files
